message.example: Provide an API key via Authorization: Bearer oas_live_... or X-API-Key header

expiration.description: Optional: specific expiration date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to nearest.

modelParams.description:
Model-specific calibration options. Supported keys by model:

**heston:** `accuracyMode` ("fast" | "balanced" | "precise", default "balanced")

**sabr:** `fixBeta` (boolean, default true), `accuracyMode` ("fast" | "balanced" | "precise")

**jd:** `modelType` / `jdModel` ("kou" | "merton" | "bates", default "kou")

**vg / localvol:** no extra options

ivRmse.description: Aggregate IV RMSE across all options used for the fit.

nOptions.description: Number of options included in the fit.

CalibrationQuotaError.description: Returned by /v1/compute/calibrate when the user exceeds the per-day calibration cap. Separate from the general rate limit — calibrations are hard-capped because each run can consume 5-60 seconds of CPU.

limit.description: Hard cap for the scope (500 per day)

resetsAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the quota window resets (UTC)

used.description: Number of successful calibrations the user has made in the scope window

EodExposureResponse.description: Compact end-of-day dealer-positioning levels for one symbol, computed over a single 0-60 DTE universe so every field agrees: gamma magnet, gamma flip, call/put walls, dealer regime, net GEX/DEX, 30-day expected move, and the top strikes by gamma contribution. The window, units, and schemaVersion travel with every row. Returns 404 when no usable EOD option data exists for the symbol/date (e.g. thinly-traded names or futures symbols, which are not covered).

asOf.description: When the pipeline computed this row.

date.description: EOD session the levels are computed from.

dealerRegime.description: Sign of net gamma at spot over the window.

dteWindow.description: Expiration window every level is computed over.

expectedMovePct30d.description: 30-day ATM-implied move as a decimal fraction.

gammaMagnet.description: Strike with the largest absolute net gamma in the window.

netDex.description: Net dollar delta exposure over the window (see units).

netGex.description: Net dollar gamma exposure over the window (see units).

schemaVersion.description: Contract version; bumped on any breaking shape change.

topContributingStrikes.description: Strikes contributing most to net gamma, ordered by absolute gamma contribution.

topContributingStrikesLimit.description: Max number of rows returned in topContributingStrikes.

units.description: Plain-language units for the exposure and expected-move fields.

atmIv.description: Alias for `atmIV`. Accepted so callers can pipe the `atmIv` field from /v1/data/metrics/{symbol} or /v1/data/snapshot/{symbol} directly.

ExposureFullResult.description: Full result: compact snapshot plus the entire by-strike Greek-exposure array.

ExposureResponse.description: Default: ExposureFullResult ({ snapshot, byStrike }). With ?detail=snapshot: ExposureSnapshot directly.

ExposureSnapshot.description: Compact exposure summary: net totals, walls, gamma flip, abs gamma anchor, regime, top-10 strikes.

absGamma.description: Strike with the largest absolute net gamma in the wall/flip universe

topStrikes.description: Top-10 strikes by absolute net gamma

ExposureStrike.description: Per-strike Greek-exposure row. Dealer-perspective dollar exposures across the full chain.

FitDiagnostics.description: Per-moneyness-bucket fit-quality diagnostics. Computed by re-pricing each market option with the calibrated parameters. Currently emitted for `model: "heston"` only; other models will gain support as their public price functions are exposed.

bucketCount.description: Number of options included in each moneyness bucket, mirrors `bucketRmse`.

bucketRmse.description: RMSE of price residuals within each moneyness bucket. Null when the bucket has no eligible options.

residuals.description: Per-option residuals (model price − market mid). Capped at 100, sorted by descending |residual| so the worst fits survive the cap.

worstFitOption.description: Single worst-fitting option in absolute residual terms. Null if no options were re-priceable.

moneyness.description: K / S at the time of calibration.

priceResidual.description: Model price − market mid (in price units, NOT IV units — aggregate `fitError.ivRmse` is the IV-space companion).

GreeksResponse.description: Full 17-Greek response for all 17 pricing models. Analytical or native Greeks are used where available; finite-difference bump-and-reprice fills the remaining sensitivities. Monte Carlo Greeks use parallel bump-and-reprice over price-only Monte Carlo evaluations. Exotic models may optionally include an `exoticInsight` block when `includeInsight: true` is requested.

lambda.description: Leverage: delta × S / P (also called Omega)

warnings.description: Resolution warnings emitted during ticker auto-fill

HestonModelGreeks.description: Sensitivities of the Heston price to its five model parameters. Present only when `model: "heston"` was requested. Computed by symmetric finite-difference around the calibrated parameter point; useful for assessing exposure to a miscalibrated Heston model (vs. classic Greeks which assume the model is correct and only the market inputs vary).

dV0.description: d(price) / d(V0) — sensitivity to instantaneous variance.

atm_iv_7d.description: 7-day tenor ATM IV — closest persisted proxy to event-week vol around earnings.

iv.description: Unified IV — smv → callIV → putIV fallback. Null only if all three sources are missing.

yte.description: Years to expiration as a decimal (calendar-day fraction).

IVSurfaceResponse.description: Strike × expiration grid of implied volatility for the symbol. Each row carries the smoothed value (`smv`), the call-side and put-side mid IVs, and a unified `iv` that falls back through smv → callIV → putIV. Any of the IV fields may be null when source data is missing.

date.description: Market date the surface snapshot applies to.

expirations.description: Sorted list of distinct expiration dates present in `data`.

rowCount.description: Number of strike × expiration rows returned.

spotPrice.description: Spot price observed for this surface, in dollars.

ticker.description: Underlying symbol echoed from the request (uppercased).

MaxPainCurveRow.description: One per-strike row of the max-pain curve. Pain values are dollar-weighted aggregate OI × distance × multiplier.

McDistribution.description: Terminal underlying-price distribution from a Monte Carlo simulation. Present only when `model: "mc"` and `detail: "distribution"` (or `"full"`). Computed from every simulated path — does not subsample.

count.description: Number of simulated paths the distribution was built from.

histogram.description: Equal-width histogram. `binEdges` length is `counts.length + 1`; bin `i` covers `[binEdges[i], binEdges[i+1])`. Bin count is configurable up to a server-enforced cap of 200; default 50.

mean.description: Mean of the simulated terminal price.

percentiles.description: Linearly-interpolated percentiles of the simulated terminal price.

McStats.description: Monte Carlo simulation statistics. Present only when `model: "mc"`. Use `stdError` and the (`ciLow`, `ciHigh`) 95% confidence interval to gauge convergence — increase `nPaths` if the interval is wider than your tolerance.

ciHigh.description: Upper bound of the 95% confidence interval for the price.

ciLow.description: Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval for the price.

nPaths.description: Effective number of paths the estimate was built from. With antithetic variates enabled this is up to 2× the requested `nPaths`.

stdError.description: Standard error of the price estimate.

MetricsResponse.description: Per-symbol EOD metrics row. Sourced from option_ticker_snapshots.

error.example: No data found for this symbol

fullPaths.description: Raw simulated paths. Present only when `model: "mc"` and `detail: "full"`. Subsampled uniformly to satisfy two server-enforced caps: a max path count (default 1,000,000) and a max total numbers serialized (default 5,000,000). `fullPathsTruncated: true` flags when subsampling ran.

fullPathsTruncated.description: True when the response was uniformly subsampled because the simulated path count exceeded the cap.

greeks.description: Per-Greek values produced as a side effect of pricing for some models (binomial returns delta/gamma; PDE production path returns delta/gamma/vega/theta/rho). Use /v1/compute/greeks for the full 17-Greek response across all models.

warnings.description: Resolution warnings emitted during ticker auto-fill

RegimeResponse.description: Latest market regime. Both `daily` and `intraday` are raw rows from the regime_daily / regime_intraday warehouse tables. Field set is owned by the data-pipeline team and may evolve over time — access defensively. Either field may be null when no row is available.

daily.description: Latest row from regime_daily for symbol=MARKET, scope=market, ordered by market_date desc. Null when no row exists.

intraday.description: Latest row from regime_intraday for symbol=MARKET, scope=market, ordered by (market_date, scan_time) desc. Null when no row exists.

ResolvedInputs.description: Echo of the inputs actually used for the calculation, after ticker auto-fill and calibration resolution.

calibrationId.description: 30-second in-memory handle returned by /v1/compute/calibrate. Persist `modelParams` for durable reuse.

modelParams.description: Model-specific knobs supplied or filled in by calibration

ResolvedSummary.description: Per-input resolution metadata when ticker auto-fill ran. Each entry is { value, source }.

ScenarioMatrixEntry.description: One cell in the scenario matrix: option value and P/L at a spot and volatility perturbation.

pnl.description: Dollar P/L versus the base option price.

pnlPercent.description: Percent P/L versus the base option price.

spot.description: Scenario spot price after applying spotChange.

matrix.description: 2D matrix of structured cells indexed by [spotChange][volChange].

SensitivityPoint.description: One point on a sensitivity sweep. `x` is the sweep variable (spot price for axis="spot", days-to-expiry for "time", volatility for "volatility"). The standard 17 Greeks come from Black-Scholes; `modelGreeks` is present only when `model: "heston"`.

x.description: Sweep variable value at this point.

SensitivityResponse.description: Per-axis sweep of Greeks. Each point carries the full 17-Greek set under Black-Scholes (model defaults to "bs"). When `model: "heston"`, points additionally carry a `modelGreeks` block with derivatives w.r.t. the Heston parameters (V0, kappa, theta, volOfVol, rho).

SnapshotResponse.description: EOD options snapshot: spot, IV term structure, GEX/DEX/HV, max pain, plus opaque curve payloads consumed by the web app charts.

chainData.description: Recent option chain rows; opaque payload

maxPainCurve.description: Max-pain curve series for the chart UI: per-strike rows of { strike, callPain, putPain, totalPain }.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key authentication. Pass via `Authorization: Bearer oas_live_...` or `X-API-Key` header.

ApiKeyHeader.description: Alternative: pass API key via `X-API-Key` header instead of `Authorization`.
ApiKeyHeader.name: X-API-Key

info.description:
Institutional-grade options analytics API. Programmatic access to 17 pricing models (including 7 exotic options), auto-calibration, real-time exposure analytics, WebSocket streaming, and pre-computed market data.

## Features

- **17 pricing models** — 10 standard (Black-Scholes, Heston, SABR, FFT (Carr-Madan with 6 characteristic functions), Variance Gamma, Jump Diffusion (Kou/Bates/Merton), Binomial, PDE, Monte Carlo, Local Volatility (Dupire + eSSVI)) plus 7 exotic (Barrier, Asian, Lookback, Digital, Compound, Chooser, Multi-Asset) with optional strategy-insight card via `includeInsight`
- **Auto-calibration** — Heston, SABR, VG, JD, and Local Vol calibrate against live broker chain data in 0.25-16s
- **Greek exposure** — GEX, DEX, VEX, Vanna, Charm, Vomma with gamma flip detection, call/put walls, abs gamma anchor, dealer positioning regime
- **Real-time streaming** — WebSocket endpoint for live quotes, Greeks, and computed exposure snapshots
- **Pre-computed data** — market regime, IV surfaces, snapshots, metrics, scanner, risk-neutral + lognormal probability distributions

## Authentication

All `/v1/*` endpoints require an API key. Pass it via either header:

- `Authorization: Bearer oas_live_...`
- `X-API-Key: oas_live_...`

## Rate limits

Separate buckets for data and compute endpoints (per user, shared across all API keys):

- **Data** (`/v1/data/*`): **120 requests/minute**
- **Compute** (`/v1/compute/*`): **120 requests/minute**
- **Cheap compute** (BS, binomial, exposure, max pain, expected move): max **6 concurrent**
- **Heavy compute** (MC, PDE, Heston, Local Vol, SABR, JD, VG, scenario, sensitivity): max **2 concurrent**
- **Calibration**: max **1 concurrent** job (own lane, separate from compute pools) · hard-capped at **500/day** per user
- Heavy compute endpoints may return **503** when worker capacity is unavailable
- 429 responses include `X-RateLimit-Bucket` header (`data` or `compute`)

## Broker credentials (BYOK)

Some endpoints (calibration, full-mode probability, streaming) require live broker chain data. The API doesn't pay for market data — you supply your own credentials via headers:

- **TastyTrade**: `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade`, `X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token`, `X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret`
- **Tradier**: `X-Broker-Type: tradier`, `X-Tradier-Key`

Note: Public.com is **not supported on the API tier** (their Individual API program restricts third-party platforms). Public is available on the Pro webapp tier for in-browser use only.

## Best practices (especially for LLM-driven agents)

- **Calibrate once, then persist the fitted params — not the `calibrationId`.** `calibrationId` is a short-lived handle into a 30-second in-memory cache; it works for immediate follow-up `/price` and `/greeks` calls but is not durable. For reuse beyond 30s, save the fitted parameters from the calibration response and pass them as `modelParams` on subsequent calls. Either path avoids the 500/day calibration quota. Don't set `resolve.calibration: true` on every repeated call for the same symbol.
- **Prefer `/v1/data/*` over recomputing.** Greeks history, IV surfaces, snapshot metrics, metrics-batch, scanner rankings, and regime classifications are precomputed or cached where available — typically much faster than recomputing from scratch. Don't loop `/v1/compute/greeks` to reconstruct what `/v1/data/greeks-history/:symbol` already returns.
- **Use batch endpoints.** `/v1/data/metrics/batch` takes multiple symbols in one call; MultiAsset accepts `modelParams.tickers` for basket pricing. One batch call beats a loop of N single-symbol calls.
- **Use ticker auto-fill instead of fabricated numerics.** Pass `symbol` + `expiry` and let the API resolve `S`, `sigma`, `r`, `t` from cached market data — the response's `resolved` block shows which source filled each field (`snapshot`, `atmIv-expiry`, `hv-30d`, `fred-DGS3MO`, …). Don't hallucinate market values from training data.
- **Always inspect `warnings` and `resolved`.** The compute route returns top-level `warnings` plus a `resolved` block whenever ticker auto-fill ran. Agents that ignore these can act on a price computed with a stale spot or a missing-dividend fallback.
- **Honor `Retry-After` on 429.** Sleep the suggested duration. Retrying instantly in a tight loop wastes tokens and bounces off the same limit.
- **Use `X-Cache-Status` to guide polling cadence.** Data responses include `X-Cache-Status`; values include `HIT`, `MISS`, `DEDUP`, `NO_REDIS`, and error/fallback statuses. If you see all HITs, you're polling faster than the underlying data updates — back off.
- **Don't repoll faster than the underlying data updates.** Most `/v1/data/*` endpoints back tables that refresh on a documented daily cron. Repolling every 30s returns the same bytes.
- **Stream live data over `/v1/ws`, don't poll REST.** Up to 30 msg/sec × 50 symbols × 5 sessions beats any REST polling loop on freshness and rpm budget.
- **Match model to task.** Black-Scholes and Binomial are sub-100ms and correct for vanilla options. Reserve Heston/SABR/Local Vol/JD/MC for cases where skew, jumps, or path-dependence actually matter.
- **Scope and secure keys.** Create compute-only, data-only, or streaming-only keys to limit blast radius. Never embed keys in client-side JS, browser extensions, public repos, or LLM tool manifests.
info.title: Options Analysis Suite API

get.description: Unauthenticated health probe. Returns current service status and uptime.

post.description:
Fetches the live options chain from the specified broker (BYOK credentials via headers) and calibrates model parameters. Returns fitted parameters + calibration ID for reuse in `/price` and `/greeks` via the `calibrationId` field. Supports Heston, SABR, VG, JD, and Local Volatility (eSSVI multi-expiration surface).

### Required headers by broker

- **Tradier** (1 credential header) — uses a static Production Access Token:
  - `X-Broker-Type: tradier`
  - `X-Tradier-Key: <your Tradier production access token>`
- **Tastytrade** (2 credential headers) — uses OAuth 2.0 refresh-token flow. The server posts `{grant_type: "refresh_token", refresh_token, client_secret}` to `https://api.tastyworks.com/oauth/token` to mint a short-lived access token, which is reused until near expiry; separately, calibration results themselves are cached for 30 seconds. Both credential values must come from the **same Tastytrade OAuth app** — the `client_secret` identifies which app issued the refresh token, so dropping either header fails the OAuth exchange:
  - `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade`
  - `X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token: <OAuth refresh token from your Tastytrade OAuth app>`
  - `X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret: <OAuth client secret from the SAME Tastytrade OAuth app>`

Public.com is not supported on the API tier (their Individual API program restricts third-party platforms). Web-app Pro users can still use Public directly in the browser.

**Quotas:** Calibrations are hard-capped at **500 per day** per user (in addition to the 120/min compute rate limit). Over-quota returns `429 Too Many Requests` with a `Retry-After` header and a `CalibrationQuotaError` body. The quota window resets at midnight UTC. Cached calibration responses (30-second TTL per `credentials.type + symbol + model + expiration + modelParams` key) still count against the quota.

parameters.description: Which broker to fetch chain data from. Public.com is not supported on the API tier.
parameters.name: X-Broker-Type

parameters.description: Required when `X-Broker-Type: tradier`. Your Tradier production access token (https://docs.tradier.com/docs/getting-started).
parameters.name: X-Tradier-Key

parameters.description: Required when `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade` (alongside `X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret`). OAuth refresh token issued by your Tastytrade OAuth app. See https://developer.tastytrade.com/oauth/ for the grant flow.
parameters.name: X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token

parameters.description: Required when `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade` (alongside `X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token`). OAuth client secret for your Tastytrade OAuth app. Used with the refresh token to mint a short-lived access token per calibration.
parameters.name: X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret

heston.summary: Heston calibration on live SPY chain

400.description: Bad request — validation failed or invalid parameters

403.description: API key lacks the required scope for this endpoint

429.description: Rate limit OR calibration quota exceeded. Inspect the response body: if it has a `code: "CALIBRATION_QUOTA_EXCEEDED"` field, the user hit the daily calibration cap (500/day, resets midnight UTC). Otherwise it is a standard per-minute rate limit.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the client can retry (rate limit window or quota window reset)

503.description: Service unavailable — worker pool draining or broker connection failed

post.summary: Auto-calibrate model parameters from live chain data

post.description:
Estimate the expected move of the underlying using the ATM straddle method with the industry-standard 85% adjustment factor. Also returns an IV-based approximation for comparison.

**Field naming:** accepts either `atmIV` (uppercase V, canonical) **or** `atmIv` (lowercase v) for the ATM implied volatility input. `atmIv` matches the casing returned by `/v1/data/metrics/{symbol}` and `/v1/data/snapshot/{symbol}`, so you can pipe those outputs straight in. Both fields are equivalent; if both are supplied, `atmIV` wins.

**Tip:** the `/v1/data/metrics/{symbol}` response already includes `expectedMovePct` precomputed daily — reach for this endpoint only when you need a custom `atmIV` / `daysToExpiry` / `strikePrice` combination.

400.description: Bad request — validation failed or invalid parameters

403.description: API key lacks the required scope for this endpoint

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. X-RateLimit-* headers indicate current limits.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit resets

post.description: Compute full Greek exposure from a strike list: net gamma/delta/vega/vanna/charm/vomma, call/put walls, gamma flip, abs gamma anchor, dealer positioning regime, and per-strike top contributors. Default response is the full result `{ snapshot, byStrike }`; pass `?detail=snapshot` to receive only the compact `ExposureSnapshot` directly.

parameters.description: Omit (default) for the full `{ snapshot, byStrike }` result; pass `snapshot` to receive only the compact ExposureSnapshot.
parameters.name: detail

example.summary: Default response: full result ({ snapshot, byStrike }). Pass ?detail=snapshot to receive only the inner snapshot directly.

200.description: Exposure result. By default an `ExposureFullResult` ({ snapshot, byStrike }); when `?detail=snapshot` is passed, an `ExposureSnapshot` directly.

400.description: Bad request — validation failed or invalid parameters

403.description: API key lacks the required scope for this endpoint

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. X-RateLimit-* headers indicate current limits.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit resets

post.description:
Compute all 17 Greeks for any of the 17 supported pricing models. Returns price alongside the full Greek vector. Accepts the same `modelParams` as `/v1/compute/price`, including ticker auto-fill (`symbol`, `expiry`, `modelParams.tickers`, `resolve.calibration`). Set `includeInsight: true` to attach the Strategy-page exotic insight card for supported exotic models (Barrier, Asian, Lookback, Digital, Compound, Chooser, MultiAsset).

**Required tenor:** every request must specify a time-to-expiry via either `t` (numeric years) or `expiry` / `expiration` (YYYY-MM-DD). Missing both returns a `400` validation error — the API never invents a tenor.

hestonFromTicker.summary: Heston from ticker (auto-calibration, requires broker headers)

exoticInsight.subtitle: Should I DCA or lump sum? Should I keep rolling covered calls?

exoticInsight.subtitle: Where should I set my stop-loss?

exoticInsight.subtitle: How does correlation affect value? What if correlation changes?

post.description: Compute the max pain strike for an options chain — the strike at which the total outstanding option value (calls + puts) is minimized. Returns the max pain strike plus the full pain distribution across all strikes.

post.description:
Price an option using one of 17 supported pricing models. For advanced models, pass model-specific parameters via `modelParams`, or use a `calibrationId` from `/v1/compute/calibrate` to reuse calibrated parameters.

**Required tenor:** every request must specify a time-to-expiry. Provide either `t` (numeric years) **or** `expiry` / `expiration` (YYYY-MM-DD). Missing both returns a `400` validation error.

**Ticker auto-fill:** supply `symbol: "AAPL"` (plus `expiry: "YYYY-MM-DD"` since tenor is mandatory) to let the API fill `S`, `sigma`, `q`, `r`, and `t` from cached market data. User-supplied numeric fields always win; the response `resolved` block reports the effective value + source (`override`, `snapshot`, `atmIv-expiry`, `hv-30d`, `dividends-ttm`, `fred-DGS3MO`, etc.). For MultiAsset, supply `modelParams.tickers: ["SPY","QQQ",...]` to build the basket from 5y monthly correlations. Requires both `compute` and `data` scopes on the API key. Set `resolve.calibration: true` (Heston/SABR/VG/JD/LocalVol only) to additionally run auto-calibration under the existing calibration quota — broker headers (`X-Broker-Type`, `X-Tradier-Key`, …) are required for this mode.

**modelParams by model:**

- **heston:** `v0`, `kappa` / `hestonKappa`, `theta` / `hestonTheta`, `volOfVol` / `xi` / `hestonVolOfVol` (legacy alias: `sigma`), `rho` / `hestonRho`, `integrationPoints` (legacy alias: `hestonOrder`)
- **sabr:** `sabrAlpha` / `alpha`, `sabrBeta` / `beta`, `sabrRho` / `rho`, `sabrNu` / `nu`, `approximationMethod` (hagan | obloj | paulot, default hagan), `shift`, `expansionOrder`, `useSmallTimeCorrection`, `fixBeta`, `fixedBetaValue`, `atmReference`, `volFloor`, `useArbitrageChecks`
- **fft:** `processType` (blackScholes | merton | varianceGamma | heston | kou | bates), `N`, `alpha`, `eta`, `integrationMethod` (legacy aliases: `fftGridSize`, `fftAlpha`, `fftEta`, `fftIntegrationMethod`). Process-specific params match the webapp: Merton `mertonLambda`, `mertonMuJ`, `mertonSigmaJ`; Variance Gamma `vgSigma`, `vgNu`, `vgTheta`; Heston `hestonV0`, `hestonKappa`, `hestonTheta`, `hestonXi`, `hestonRho`; Kou `kouLambda`, `kouP`, `kouEta1`, `kouEta2`; Bates `batesV0`, `batesKappa`, `batesTheta`, `batesSigmaV`, `batesRho`, `batesLambda`, `batesMuJ`, `batesSigmaJ`
- **vg:** `sigma` / `vgSigma`, `nu` / `vgNu`, `theta` / `vgTheta`, optional `method`, `fftPoints`, `alpha`, `eta`, `cacheFFT`, `weighting`
- **jd:** `modelType` / `jdModel` (kou | merton | bates, default kou). Merton/Kou accept `baseVolatility` as the diffusion sigma override. Kou: `lambda`, `pUp`, `etaUp`, `etaDown`, optional `tailTolerance` / `tailTol`, `maxK`, `forceFFT`, `fftGridSize`. Merton: `lambda`, `muJ`, `sigmaJ`, optional `maxPoisson`. Bates: `v0`, `kappa`, `theta`, `sigmaV` / `volOfVol` / `xi`, `rho`, `lambda`, `muJ`, `sigmaJ`, optional `forceExactCF`, `fftGridSize`
- **binomial:** `steps`, `enableAmerican` / `isAmerican`, `treeType` (CRR | JR | TIAN | LR), optional Richardson controls `useRichardsonExtrap`, `richardsonMode`, `stepMultiplier`, `extrapOrder`, and discrete-dividend inputs either as `dividends` array or the webapp-style `useDiscreteDividends`, `exDividendDates`, `dividendAmounts`. The public API uses the ex-date discrete-dividend model.
- **pde:** `forceMethod` / `pdeForceMethod`, `accuracyThreshold` / `pdeAccuracyThreshold`, plus grid controls `spaceSteps`, `timeSteps`, `sMin`, `sMax`, `gridSpacing`, `rannacherSteps`
- **mc:** `paths` / `mcPaths` / `nPaths`, `steps` / `stepsPerPath` / `timeSteps` / `nSteps`, `seed` / `mcSeed`, `process` / `processType` (`blackscholes` | `jumpdiffusion` | `meanreverting` | `heston` | `localvolcev` | `localvoldupire`; aliases such as `BlackScholes`, `Merton`, `LocalVolatility`, `Dupire`, and `CEV` are normalized), top-level process params or nested `processParams`, `useAntithetic`, `useControlVariate`, `useImportanceSampling`, `useMomentMatching`, `useQMC`, `qmcType`. `rngMethod: SobolTrue` is normalized to Sobol QMC; non-QMC browser RNG labels fall back to the standard server RNG path. Monte Carlo pricing in the API is European-only. Browser-only execution flags such as `useGPU`, `batchSize`, and `parallelThreads` are ignored server-side.
- **localvol:** PDE-only public surface. Supported params: `impliedVolData` (array or JSON string), `impliedVolDataArray`, `useDupire`, `useAmerican`, `interpolation` (essvi | linear | svi | dupire; cubic is no longer supported), `spotSteps` / `pdeGridSize`, `timeSteps` / `pdeTimeSteps`, `rannacherSteps`, `regularizationParam`, `localVolBeta` / `beta` / `cevBeta`, `cevScale`, `minVol` / `dupireMinVol`, `maxVol` / `dupireMaxVol`, `ticker`
- **barrier:** `barrierType` (up-out | down-out | up-in | down-in | double-out | double-in), `barrierLevel`, `rebate`, `monitoringType` (continuous | discrete | window), `monitoringFreq`, `upperBarrier`, `lowerBarrier`, `upperRebate`, `lowerRebate`, `windowStart`, `windowEnd`
- **asian:** `averagingType` (arithmetic | geometric), `strikeType` (fixed | floating), `method` (curran | geometric; `mc`/`montecarlo` requests are normalized to curran server-side), `numObservations`, `observationFreq`, `observationDates`, `pastFixings`, `runningAverage`, `runningSum`, `fixingsElapsed`
- **lookback:** `lookbackType` (floating | fixed), `currentMax`, `currentMin`, `monitoringType` / `monitoring`, `monitoringFrequency`, `method`
- **digital:** `type` / `digitalType` — accepts either short form (`cash` | `asset` | `gap`) or long form (`cash-or-nothing` | `asset-or-nothing` | `gap`); both are equivalent server-side. Also `cashAmount`, `gapAmount`, `smoothing` (clamped server-side to a minimum of 1e-4), `method`
- **compound:** `compoundType` (call-on-call | call-on-put | put-on-call | put-on-put), `underlyingStrike`, `firstExpiry`, `secondExpiry` (defaults to request `t`), `method`, `mcPaths`, `mcSeed`, `useAntithetic`, `useControlVariate`
- **chooser:** `chooserType` (simple | complex), `choiceTime`, `maturity`, `callStrike`, `putStrike`, `callMaturity`, `putMaturity`, `method`, `mcPaths`, `useAntithetic`, `useControlVariate`
- **multiasset:** `payoffType` / `type` / `multiAssetType` (basket | spread | max | min), `numAssets`, `correlation`, `corrMatrix` / `correlationMatrix`, `prices[]`, `volatilities[]`, `dividends[]`, `weights[]`, `method`, `mcPaths`, `seed`, `useAntithetic`, `useControlVariate`, `useSobol`. If `prices[]` is omitted, the request spot `S` is used as the first asset price.

post.description:
Compute the implied probability distribution of the underlying at expiration. Returns BOTH the risk-neutral distribution (eSSVI + Breeden-Litzenberger with finite-difference fallback) AND a lognormal baseline, plus statistics (implied move, range bounds, fair value). Supports two modes:

- **simple**: Pre-computed strikes + IVs (no broker needed)
- **full**: BYOK broker credentials; API fetches live chain across multiple expirations and calibrates eSSVI

### Required headers for `mode: "full"`

- **Tradier** (1 credential header) — uses a static Production Access Token:
  - `X-Broker-Type: tradier`
  - `X-Tradier-Key: <your Tradier production access token>`
- **Tastytrade** (2 credential headers) — uses OAuth 2.0 refresh-token flow. The server posts `{grant_type: "refresh_token", refresh_token, client_secret}` to Tastytrade's token endpoint to mint a short-lived access token, which is reused until near expiry. Both values must come from the **same Tastytrade OAuth app**:
  - `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade`
  - `X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token: <OAuth refresh token from your Tastytrade OAuth app>`
  - `X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret: <OAuth client secret from the SAME Tastytrade OAuth app>`

`mode: "simple"` requires no headers. Public.com is not supported on the API tier.

Credential sources: Tradier → https://docs.tradier.com/docs/getting-started · Tastytrade → https://developer.tastytrade.com/oauth/

parameters.description: Required for `mode: full`. Public.com is not supported on the API tier (their Individual API program restricts third-party platforms).
parameters.name: X-Broker-Type

parameters.description: Required for `mode: full` with `X-Broker-Type: tradier`. Your Tradier production access token (https://docs.tradier.com/docs/getting-started).
parameters.name: X-Tradier-Key

parameters.description: Required for `mode: full` with `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade` (alongside `X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret`). OAuth refresh token from your Tastytrade OAuth app (https://developer.tastytrade.com/oauth/).
parameters.name: X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token

parameters.description: Required for `mode: full` with `X-Broker-Type: tastytrade` (alongside `X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token`). OAuth client secret for your Tastytrade OAuth app.
parameters.name: X-Tastytrade-Client-Secret

full.description: Requires broker credentials via headers (X-Broker-Type, X-Tastytrade-Refresh-Token, etc.)
full.summary: Full mode — fetches live chain from broker

get.description:
Quarterly earnings history, EPS and revenue surprises, and next earnings date. Sourced from `earnings_data` table.

**Options analytics (`?includeOptionsAnalytics=true`)** — attach an `optionsAnalytics` block containing realized vs implied post-earnings moves, IV crush, and aggregate stats across the last 8 quarters. Derived by joining the earnings history with `stock_prices` (close-to-close realized move) and `scan_tickers` (historical ATM IV, tenor preference 7D → 30D → generic). Adds ~2 extra database reads; the base response is unchanged when the flag is false or absent.

**Windowing:** BMO (before-open) reports use prior-day close → report-day close. AMC or unknown-timing reports use report-day close → next-trading-day close. The response `quarters[].time` echoes the source `time` tag when available (currently often `null`, which falls through to AMC-style windowing).

**`impliedMovePct`** is a 1-trading-day approximation: `preEventAtmIv × √(1/252) × 100`. Event vol concentrates in the event-week expiration, so this often understates what options actually priced in; use it as a lower bound. `realizedOverImplied > 1` means the stock moved more than options priced in. `ivCrushPct` is `(preIv − postIv) / preIv × 100`.

parameters.description: When `true`, attach `optionsAnalytics` with per-quarter realized/implied move, IV crush, and aggregate stats (last 8 reported quarters).
parameters.name: includeOptionsAnalytics

ivTenorUsed.description: Which IV tenor satisfied the pre-event lookup.

preEventAtmIv.description: Decimal IV at pre-event close (e.g. 0.42 for 42%).

time.description: Report timing if the source tagged it; unknown/unrecognized values are normalized to null (falls through to AMC-style windowing).

upPct.description: Share of quarters with non-negative close-to-close move.

get.description:
Market-wide monthly dealer block-trade summary from FINRA. Not per-symbol — each row is a dealer (`mpid`) × month × block-size bucket (`summary_type_code`). Returns shares, trades, block counts, average block size, and ATS/block percentages. Defaults to the latest month when `month` is omitted. Filter by `mpid` or `summary_type`.

**Row shape note:** monthly totals (`total_trades`, `total_shares`, `avg_trade_size`, `ats_trade_pct`, `ats_share_pct`) repeat across the six size-bucket rows for the same dealer-month — that mirrors how FINRA ships the data. Bucket-specific fields (`block_count`, `block_shares`, `avg_block_size`, `block_trade_pct`, `block_share_pct`) vary per row. The `ats_otc` field is descriptive (today always `ATS`), not filterable, since it is not part of the table primary key. Sourced from `finra_blocks_monthly`.

parameters.description: Month start (YYYY-MM-01). Defaults to latest available.
parameters.name: month

parameters.description: Filter to a single dealer (e.g. INCR, CRD149823).
parameters.name: mpid

parameters.description: Block-size bucket: 2K = 2K–10K shares; 10K = 10K+ shares; 100K = $100K–<$200K; 200K = $200K+; combined buckets are the AND-intersection.
parameters.name: summary_type

200.description: Dealer block-summary rows sorted by block_shares desc

400.description: Bad request — validation failed or invalid parameters

403.description: API key lacks the required scope for this endpoint

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. X-RateLimit-* headers indicate current limits.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the rate limit resets

503.description: Service unavailable — worker pool draining or broker connection failed

parameters.description: API key (alternative to Authorization header for WebSocket upgrade)
parameters.name: apiKey

tags.description: Pricing, Greeks, and analytics endpoints. Multi-model (17 pricing models: 10 standard — BS, Heston, SABR, FFT, VG, JD, Binomial, PDE, Monte Carlo, Local Vol — plus 7 exotic — Barrier, Asian, Lookback, Digital, Compound, Chooser, Multi-Asset). Auto-calibration available for Heston, SABR, VG, JD, and Local Vol against live broker chain data. Exotic models support the optional includeInsight flag for strategy-level comparison metrics.
tags.name: Compute

tags.description: Pre-computed market data: regime detection, IV surfaces, snapshots, metrics, scanner results, market trends. All endpoints read from the Options Analysis Suite data warehouse.
tags.name: Data

tags.description: Real-time WebSocket streaming at `/v1/ws`. Supports quote, greeks, and exposure channels with BYOK broker credentials (TastyTrade, Tradier). See streaming documentation.
tags.name: Streaming

tags.description: Unauthenticated health check endpoint for monitoring.
tags.name: Health
